step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("recent studies on aortic arch anatomy and traumatic injury severity")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First, write 2 sentences summarizing your revised view based on new evidence. Then conclude with your answer choice in boxed format.
The anatomic fixation of the aortic arch—particularly at the isthmus just distal to the left subclavian artery due to the ligamentum arteriosum—creates mechanical vulnerability during abrupt deceleration, increasing shear stress and promoting tearing or rupture. Multiple sources consistently report that the highest frequency of traumatic aortic injury occurs precisely at this fixed segment, confirming that arch anatomy influences trauma severity.
